title: FURINA: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Patch Correctness Assessment
data:
Y Li, Y Ji, X Chen, Z Shen, J Ge, B Luo- ACM Transactions on Software Engineering, 2026
Automated Program Repair (APR) has made substantial advancements in fixing real-
world defects. However, the overfitting problem, namely the phenomenon where 
APR-generated patches pass the given test suite but fail to correctly fix the 
underlying defects, remains a major challenge. To mitigate this problem, numerous 
Automated Patch Correctness Assessment (APCA) methods have been proposed. 
Among them, learning-based APCA methods currently represent the state-of-the-art
